body.label: source.root.theorem
body.claim: Let $T : V \to V$ be a linear transformation on a finite-dimensional vector space satisfying $T^2 = T$. Let $\lambda$ be a scalar with $\lambda \neq 0,1$. Define $S = T - \lambda I$. Then $S$ is invertible.

proof_steps.claim: Let $T : V \to V$ be a linear transformation on a finite-dimensional vector space satisfying $T^2 = T$. Let $\lambda$ be a scalar with $\lambda \neq 0,1$. Define $S = T - \lambda I$. Then $S$ is invertible.
